question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: An access-denied case for a SharePoint folder was caused by incorrect group nesting in Entra ID; fixing the nesting and synchronizing restored access
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 20:
﻿Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Access to project folder denied
Ticket number: TI-1569482
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 03
Owner: Employee 18
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.02.25 09:06
Closure
Closure date: 25.02.25 14:38
Closure text: Root cause identified (group nesting in Entra ID). Permission structure cleaned up. Access restored.
Activities
Emails:
18.02.25 08:59
Subject: No access to project folder “2025_Migration”
From: Contact Person 03
To: HELPDESK
Good morning
Several employees from the team “IT-Projekte” receive the following message when accessing the SharePoint folder
/Projects/2025_Migration
Access denied – You do not have the required permissions
Access was working until last week.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 03
18.02.25 09:06
Ticket TI-1569482 created
Assigned to: Employee 18
Status: In Progress
Notes:
18.02.25 09:22 – Remote analysis
• Access tested with affected user → reproducible
• SharePoint Online reachable
• Other project folders accessible
• Only folder “2025_Migration” affected
• Permission view in SharePoint shows: Access via group “SP_Project_IT”
Comparison with working user:
This user is member of “SP_Project_IT_Lead”
Suspicion: Group membership inconsistent
18.02.25 10:05 – Entra ID review
• User is member of Azure AD group “IT-Projekte”
• This group is nested in “SP_Project_IT”
• Notable: “SP_Project_IT” was newly created last week
History checked:
Old group “SP_Project_IT_v1” was deactivated
18.02.25 11:40 – Test measure
User added directly to “SP_Project_IT”
→ Access works immediately
After removing the direct assignment → access denied again
Conclusion: Nesting not functioning correctly
19.02.25 08:55 – Further analysis
Review of group structure:
IT-Projekte
└── SP_Project_IT_v1 (deactivated)
└── SP_Project_IT (new)
“IT-Projekte” was still nested in the old group, not in the new one.
Therefore, users did not receive effective permissions on the new SharePoint folder.
19.02.25 10:20 – Adjustment
• Nesting corrected
• “IT-Projekte” properly integrated into “SP_Project_IT”
• Old group removed
• Synchronization awaited
20.02.25 – Monitoring
Multiple users tested
Access consistently possible
25.02.25 14:30 – Customer feedback
Access works again for all employees without restriction.
Ticket closure
25.02.25 14:38
Dear Contact Person 03
The root cause was incorrect group nesting in Entra ID after the creation of the new SharePoint permission group.
The structure was cleaned up and tested.
Access has been restored.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This sentence reports a past cause and remediation (what was done and its effect) rather than issuing a recommendation or instructing future action.
groundedness_reason: The ticket states the root cause was incorrect group nesting in Entra ID, documents correcting the nesting (and awaiting synchronization), and notes that access was subsequently restored, which directly supports the answer.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The SharePoint symptom you describe—one folder suddenly blocked for some users after a rename and application of a sensitivity/confidential label, with inconsistent visibility (works for some users, works in Incognito for others)—matches a prior incident that required escalation to the M365 / Identity team for in-depth analysis. In that case the label+permission interaction was investigated by a System Engineer and required tests against Azure AD groups and Purview/AIP behavior

Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Project folder “Compliance_Review” partially not visible
Ticket number: TI-1578891
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 02
Owner: Employee 27
Origin: Email

Created on: 18.03.25 08:43
Activities
18.03.25 08:36
Subject: Access to Compliance_Review folder missing for some users
From: Contact Person 02
Good morning
As part of our internal audit, we have identified that some employees do not have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Others can see it without issue.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 02
18.03.25 08:43
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 27
Status: In Progress
Notes
18.03.25 09:15
Helpdesk asks:
• Which users exactly are affected?
• Since when?
• Screenshot of error message?
• Access via browser or Teams?
18.03.25 10:02
Customer responds:
Affected users:
• M. Keller
• S. Braun
• T. Widmer
Error message:
Item not found
Access via browser (Edge).
Noticed since last week, but unclear exactly since when.
18.03.25 10:25
Helpdesk asks further:
• Are users members of the project group?
• Were permissions recently changed?
• Does it affect only this folder or others as well?
18.03.25 11:18
Customer:
We restructured several project groups last week.
New groups were created:
• COMPLIANCE_TEAM
• COMPLIANCE_EXT
Folder may have been newly inherited.
Other folders work.
18.03.25 13:05
Helpdesk checks SharePoint permissions:
• Folder has Unique Permissions
• Permission via group “SP_Compliance_Main”
• This group contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Test with working user → access OK
Test with affected user → no access
19.03.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are the users possibly assigned via multiple groups?
Were there recent role changes (HR)?
19.03.25 10:40
Customer responds:
Two users changed departments internally (Finance → Risk).
One works 50% in both teams.
Could that be relevant?
19.03.25 11:05
Helpdesk checks group memberships:
All three users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”.
No obvious differences.
19.03.25 14:55
Customer reports:
One user had access briefly today, then not again.
After restart it did not work.
20.03.25 08:20
Helpdesk asks:
Are users accessing from different devices?
Company laptop or private device?
With or without VPN?
20.03.25 09:03
Customer:
Two via company laptop
One via private device
VPN not required (cloud-only environment).
20.03.25 10:45
Helpdesk checks:
• Clear browser cache
• Test in Incognito mode
• Test in another browser
Result:
Incognito → access partially possible
20.03.25 15:10
Customer:
After signing out of all Office apps, it worked briefly.
Later not visible again.
21.03.25 08:55
Helpdesk asks:
Was the folder possibly moved or renamed?
Was the Sensitivity Label changed?
Is the document library fully loaded?
21.03.25 10:18
Customer:
Folder was renamed 2 weeks ago from “Compliance_Audit” to “Compliance_Review_2025”.
Sensitivity Label “Confidential – Internal” applied.
21.03.25 13:30
Helpdesk checks:
Label requires:
• Encryption
• Access only for group “Compliance_Core”
Notable:
“Compliance_Core” is not identical to “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”.
However, according to SharePoint, permission is still managed via “SP_Compliance_Main”.
Unclear correlation.
21.03.25 16:10
Ticket remains open due to inconsistent behavior.
Status: In Progress
22.03.25 09:05
Internal note – Employee 27
Since the behavior remains inconsistent (partial access, partial denial, partial access only in Incognito mode), the ticket is escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – M365 / Identity
22.03.25 09:12
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 11
Status: In Progress
22.03.25 10:25
Analysis by System Engineer 11
Step 1 – Sensitivity Label review
Label “Confidential – Internal” checked:
• Encryption active
• Access only for members of Azure AD group “Compliance_Core”
• Automatic inheritance for folders enabled
Notable:
Folder “Compliance_Review_2025” has Unique Permissions.
Label was applied AFTER renaming.
Hypothesis: Conflict between SharePoint permissions and AIP label encryption.
22.03.25 11:40
Test:
• User added directly to “Compliance_Core”
→ Access immediately possible
After removal from “Compliance_Core”
→ Access denied again
However:
Other users WITHOUT “Compliance_Core” membership have access.
Hypothesis rejected – not purely group-based.
24.03.25 08:55
Analysis Azure AD Sign-In Logs
For affected users:
• Token contains claim for “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• No claim for “Compliance_Core”
• Conditional Access evaluation successful
No blocking visible.
24.03.25 10:10
In-depth review via Microsoft Purview / Information Protection
is_rec_reason: The statement describes a past incident and investigative actions taken (escalation and tests) rather than offering a recommendation, suggestion, or directive for future action.
groundedness_reason: SOURCE 30 documents the renamed folder with a sensitivity label and inconsistent visibility (partial access, Incognito tests), escalation to BU Infrastructure – M365/Identity, and System Engineer 11 investigating label+permission interactions with tests involving Azure AD group membership and Microsoft Purview/Information Protection.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Parts of your ticket (Teams camera and mic disappearance) map to cases that were resolved at first-level using standard “Blueprint” checks (OS privacy permissions, app permissions, Teams restart). For example, a missing camera in Teams was fixed by enabling OS camera permissions and app access per the Blueprint
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 42:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Camera not detected
Ticket number: TI-1615042
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 09
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.10.25 09:12
Closure
Closure date: 07.10.25 11:06
Closure text: Camera available again in Teams. Root cause was missing operating system permission and blocked device setting. Steps performed according to Blueprint.
Activities
07.10.25 09:05 – Email from Contact Person 09
Good morning
For one user, the camera is no longer working in Teams. No camera is displayed in the Teams device settings. In other apps it also appears to be partially unavailable. Could you please assist?
Kind regards
07.10.25 09:12
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
07.10.25 09:30 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Could you please confirm whether the user is using Windows or Mac and whether the camera is available in the browser (e.g., Teams Web)?
Thank you.
07.10.25 09:48 – Customer response
Windows 11. The camera also does not work in the browser.
07.10.25 10:25 – Internal note
Connected to the user via remote session. The camera is visible in Device Manager, but Teams has no access. In Windows privacy settings, camera access for apps is disabled. Additionally, Teams is not enabled in the list of allowed apps.
According to “Blueprint – Teams Client Devices & Permissions – Version 1.2”, the standard checks were performed (OS privacy settings, app permissions, Teams restart).
07.10.25 10:55 – Customer feedback
User confirms that the camera is now displayed in Teams and the test call is successful.
07.10.25 11:06 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 09
The camera was blocked by operating system permissions. After adjusting the camera and app permissions according to the Blueprint, the camera is available again in Teams.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This describes previously used resolution steps and outcomes (past-tense examples) rather than issuing an explicit recommendation or instruction.
groundedness_reason: The camera claim is supported by SOURCE 42 (ticket KF52) which was resolved by adjusting OS privacy and app permissions and following the Blueprint; however the CONTEXT contains no information about a mic disappearance, so that part is unsupported.

→ Access works immediately

IT-Projekte
└── SP_Project_IT_v1 (deactivated)
└── SP_Project_IT (new)
“IT-Projekte” was still nested in the old group, not in the new one.
Therefore, users did not receive effective permissions on the new SharePoint folder.
19.02.25 10:20 – Adjustment
• Nesting corrected
• “IT-Projekte” properly integrated into “SP_Project_IT”
• Old group removed

Access consistently possible
25.02.25 14:30 – Customer feedback
Access works again for all employees without restriction.
